#  Create a Potential Change Order from an RFI 
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * **Requirements:**
    * The Change Events tool must be turned OFF in the Project Tools menu. If the Change Events tool is turned ON, Procore's workflow requires you to create a change event before you create change orders. See [Create Change Events](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/create-change-events) and [Which Procore tools can I use to create a change event?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-procore-tools-can-i-use-to-create-a-change-event) In addition, you will NOT be able to create a potential change order from an RFI. See [Create a Change Event from an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/create-a-change-event-from-an-rfi) instead of the steps below.
    * If you want to Create a Potential Change Order from an RFI, the project's Prime Contracts tool must be configured to use a multi-tier change order setting. See [Configure the Number of Prime Contract Change Order Tiers](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/prime-contracts-project/tutorials/configure-the-number-of-prime-contract-change-order-tiers).


##  Prerequisites 
  * [Create an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/create-an-rfi)
  * [Approve a Prime Contract](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/prime-contracts-project/tutorials/approve-a-prime-contract)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Select the RFI from which you want to create the potential change order.
  3. Click **Create Potential CO**. 
#####  Note
     * The **Create Potential CO** button is only visible and available when the prerequisites above have been satisfied.
     * The RFIs information is used to populate the fields in the potential change order.
  4. Add or edit information as needed.
  5. Enter a description.
  6. Add an existing change order request, create a new change order request, or do not attach a change order request.
  7. Add a change reason, who the request was received from, if there is a schedule impact, location, who a reference is, and any attachments. You can change the field change, paid in full, and void options now or later.
  8. Click **Create** to save the PCO without sending an email to members of the distribution list. Click **Create and Email** to save the PCO and send an email to members of the RFI distribution list.


## See Also
  * [Create an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/create-an-rfi)
  * [Create a Change Event from an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/create-a-change-event-from-an-rfi)
  * [Edit an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/edit-an-rfi)
title: Create a Potential Change Order from an RFI
description: How to create a Potential Change Order (PCO) from an RFI in the project's RFIs tool
